description:
You are a data-analyst on a small project.  
    Inside the workspace you already have a CSV file that contains the first-quarter sales data:

        /home/user/projects/sales_analysis/sales_q1.csv

    Content of that file (verbatim, four lines after the header):

        region,product,quantity,unit_price
        North,Widget,10,2.5
        South,Gadget,5,5.25
        East,Widget,7,2.5
        West,Gizmo,3,10.00

    Goal  
    ----  
    1. Create an SQLite database called  
       /home/user/projects/sales_analysis/sales.db  
       and import the CSV into a single table named sales (keep column names exactly as in the header).  

    2. With the SQLite command-line client, compute the total revenue per region  
       (revenue = quantity × unit_price, summed for each region).  

    3. Redirect the query output to a tab-separated text file located at  

          /home/user/projects/sales_analysis/output/region_revenue.tsv

       The directory “output” does not exist yet – create it.  

    Mandatory TSV format  
    --------------------  
    • Use TAB characters between columns, **not** spaces.  
    • The file must contain exactly five lines: one header line plus one line for each region.  
    • Lines must terminate with a single UNIX newline ‘\n’, including the last line.  
    • The header must be spelled and capitalised exactly like this:

          region	total_revenue

    • Rows must be ordered alphabetically by the region column (East, North, South, West).  
    • total_revenue values must show exactly two digits after the decimal point.  

    Example of what the file must look like (the test harness will compare it byte-for-byte):

        region	total_revenue
        East	17.50
        North	25.00
        South	26.25
        West	30.00

    You may accomplish all of this with just a handful of SQLite CLI commands.  
    When you are finished, the automated tests will check that:

      • /home/user/projects/sales_analysis/sales.db exists and contains a table called sales  
      • /home/user/projects/sales_analysis/output/region_revenue.tsv exists and matches the exact
        format and values described above.
truth:
region,product,quantity,unit_priceNorth|Widget|10|2.5South|Gadget|5|5.25East|Widget|7|2.5West|Gizmo|3|10.00
 region total_revenue East 17.50 North 25.00 South 26.25 West 30.00
